title: buildah config workingdir does not have any effect
body:
**Description**
Hi I have the follow buildah script
```
buildah --name flask-hello from f34/python3
buildah copy flask-hello apptest helloworld
buildah run flask-hello pip install -r helloworld/requirements.txt
buildah config --workingdir='helloworld' flask-hello
buildah config --cmd "python -m flask run --host 0.0.0.0" flask-hello
buildah commit flask-hello flask-hello
buildah rm flask-hello
```
the image builds fine however when I create a container with this image then flask cant find the app. When I set ls as the cmd it indeed shows its above the "helloworld" folder and not in it. the manifest clearly shows:
```
            "Cmd": [
                "python",
                "-m",
                "flask",
                "run",
                "--host",
                "0.0.0.0"
            ],
            "Image": "",
            "Volumes": null,
            "WorkingDir": "helloworld",
            "Entrypoint": [
                "container-entrypoint"
            ],

```
so it seems the workingdir is not applied correctly. What am I doing wrong here?
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. run above build script
2. create a container with ```podman run  --rm -p 5000:5000/tcp localhost/flask-hello
3. see that flask cant find the app

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.21.0
Go Version:      go1.16.3
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.12.0
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

```
I am running on coreos

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux coreos-test 5.12.9-300.fc34.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Jun 3 13:51:40 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body: I have found the mistake. The workingdir needs to be a full path so in my case for fedora its ```/opt/app-root/src/helloworld```
